name: College of Allied and Medical Professions
description: The College of Allied and Medical Professions prepares health professionals through rigorous programs in allied and medical fields, producing competent and compassionate graduates ready to serve.
vision: The College of Allied Medical Professions envisions to help in the promotion of optimal health and function through the application of scientific principles to effectively meet the existing and emerging health care needs.
mission: The College aims to prepare competent and compassionate graduates who can provide professional health care in the local and global community.

programs.name: Bachelor of Science in Medical Technology (BSMT)
programs.description: The Bachelor of Science in Medical Technology (BSMT) degree program aims to:
curriculum: Demonstrate technical competence and critical thinking in the performance of clinical laboratory tests in aid of diagnosis, treatment, management of diseases vis-à-vis biosafety and waste management;
Participate in community-oriented activities;
Provide health-related information for effective interpersonal collaborative setting;
Demonstrate proficiency in oral, written and other forms of communication;
Demonstrate inter-personal skills, leadership qualities and ethical practice of the profession;
Apply research skills in relevant areas of Medical Technology/Medical Laboratory Science practice;
Develop entrepreneurial and management skills necessary to manage a laboratory or contribute to the setting-up of a laboratory;
Maximize the use of innovative technology in the practice of the profession; and
Advocate love of country and service to the Filipino people.

programs.name: Bachelor of Science in Occupational Therapy (BSOT)
programs.description: The Bachelor of Science in Occupational Therapy (BSOT) degree program aims to:
curriculum: Synthesize and utilize knowledge of health, social, psycho-social, physical, natural and environmental sciences to the practice of occupational therapy;
Utilize occupational therapy foundations and concepts in assessment and intervention in various occupational practice settings and across lifespan within a broad continuum of care;
Demonstrate beginning management and leadership skills in various occupational therapy practice settings.
Demonstrate beginning research-related skills in the performance of various roles, to use in best OT practices for quality outcomes;
Engage actively in the advocacies of the OT profession and its stakeholders, to respond to the occupational needs of society;
Promote community health and well-being in the practice of the profession and community mobilization;
Recognize the need for, and engage in, lifelong learning activities, for personal or continuing professional development;
Work effectively and collaboratively with clients and/or in a team of inter-professional practice settings;
Demonstrate proficiency in oral and written communication skills, for interprofessional education or to educate an individual client or population;
Demonstrate social, professional responsibility and accountability; and high standards of OT ethical practice and behaviors in all settings and situations;
Demonstrate genuine service to humanity rooted in Christian faith and principles;
Contribute to the improvement of health and well-being of Capampangan communities through holistic Occupational Therapy services

programs.name: Bachelor of Science in Pharmacy (BSPh)
programs.description: The Bachelor of Science in Pharmacy (BSPh) degree program aims to:
curriculum: Adhere to legal requirements, practice standards and code of ethics for the pharmacy profession.

Apply knowledge & skills of mathematics, health sciences, social sciences, psycho-social sciences, physical sciences, environmental sciences, natural sciences, information technology and other advanced technologies to the practice of pharmacy.

Apply suitable techniques and principles in the compounding, storage, manufacturing, distribution, administration and disposal of pharmaceutical (biologicals) products to ensure quality, safety & efficacy of products.
Develop a drug therapy plan appropriate for the patient based on the evaluated patient's health and medication history.
Provide relevant information in dispensing pharmaceutical products in accordance with practice standards.
Monitor the patient's progress in achieving specific outcomes and optimize the patient's health-related quality of life.
Collaborate effectively with the patients and other members of the health care team in accordance with practice standards.
Conduct and participate in research activities in accordance to scientific research guidelines and dissemination of findings for utilization.
Demonstrate efficient and effective management skills in any organization.
Demonstrate entrepreneurial skills while ensuring patient safety.
Facilitate learning of selected learners for specific purposes (patients, students, peers, support staff, other health professionals)
Communicate effectively using culturally-appropriate language.

programs.name: Bachelor of Science in Clinical Pharmacy
programs.description: Bachelor of Science in Clinical Pharmacy degree program aims to:
curriculum: Provide clinical skills to assess the status of the patient’s health problems and determine whether the prescribed medications are optimally meeting the patient’s needs and goals of therapeutic care and evaluate the appropriateness and effectiveness of the patient’s medications.
Equip students to recognize untreated health problems that could be improved or resolved with appropriate medication therapy.

Train students to communicate and consult with patient’s physicians in selecting the medication therapy for an effectively overall therapeutic regimen, and support the health care team’s efforts to educate the patient on how to improve or maintain health, such as exercise, diet, and preventive steps like immunization and other health care providers.
Provide skills in referring patient to his or her physician or other health professionals to address specific health, wellness, or social services concerns as they arise.

Apply specialized knowledge of the scientific and clinical use of medications, including medication action, dosing, adverse effects, and drug interactions, in performing their patient care activities in collaboration with other members of the health care team.

programs.name: Bachelor of Science in Radiologic Technology (BSRT)
programs.description: The Bachelor of Science in Radiologic Technology (BSRT) degree program aims to:
curriculum: Provide students with the necessary theoretical knowledge and technical skills in order to become competent and professional radiologic technologists;

Promote awareness and develop a sense of responsibility among students as to the benefits derived from, and the risks involved in, the application of various imaging energies (e.g. x-rays, electromagnetic radiation, sound waves, etc.) to humans for diagnostic, therapeutic, or research purposes;
Instill among students the correct moral aptitude and Christian values that will guide them when dealing with patients that require their services and assistance as radiologic technologists; and
Encourage students to participate in scientific research and participate in activities designed to promote technologic advancement in the field of Radiologic Sciences.

programs.name: Bachelor of Science in Physical Therapy (BSPT)
programs.description: The Bachelor of Science in Physical Therapy (BSPT) degree program aims to:
curriculum: Direct students in providing the country with humane and scientifically proficient physical therapists who are responsive to the changing health care needs of the society;

Produce physical therapists who are competent to fulfil professional responsibilities in patient/client care, education, administration and management, lifelong learning, health promotion, community service, and research;
Provide the students with varied opportunities relevant to the following roles as a clinician, educator, administrator, researcher, advocate and community-based therapist;
Promote Christian and moral teaching-learning principles in different learning environments; and
Encourage and inspire students to partake in activities related to promoting the profession and appreciate the value of professional development.
BS Physical Therapy – Professional Enhancement Program

goals: Imbibe in the students sound moral values and Christian virtues needed in the delivery of quality health care services;
Provide students with high quality knowledge and skills needed in the practice of their respective professions;
Provide students a variety of learning opportunities relevant in the rendition of professional health care services;
Encourage students to accept responsibilities, uphold professional ethics and develop empathy and compassion for the benefit of their patients; and
Motivate and inspire students to undertake continuing education and research that will foster life-long professional growth.

studentOrganizations.name: Junior Philippine Pharmacists Association (JPPhA)
studentOrganizations.description: The JPPhA is an organization of Pharmacy students of the Department of Pharmacy. The association believes in the broad principles of pharmaceutical advancement, community involvement and research. It prepares Pharmacy students, through life long dedication and conscious efforts, to meet the challenges of the expanding role of the pharmacist and to direct health care expertise toward improving the community.

studentOrganizations.name: Physical Therapy Students Society (PTSS)
studentOrganizations.description: The Physical Therapy Students Society spearheads student projects and activities of the Department of Physical Therapy. It fosters unity and camaraderie among BS Physical Therapy students as it promotes the interests of the CAMP family in general. It conducts civic-oriented activities and training opportunities in support of formal classroom education in order to contribute to the intellectual and social growth of each student.

studentOrganizations.name: Achievers Guild
studentOrganizations.description: The Achievers Guild envisions to instill academic excellence, diligence, and compassion among students. The organization aims to intensify the students’ potentials and skills through active participation in the conduct of college-based and university-wide projects.

studentOrganizations.name: Society of Future Medical Technologists (SFMT)
studentOrganizations.description: The SFMT aims to promote unity and develop the leadership skills of BS Medical Technology students through active participation in community services and projects. It aims to instill in the hearts of future medical lab scientists a deep sense of social responsibility and compassion for a more responsive and relevant cause.

studentOrganizations.name: AlliMEd Explorer
studentOrganizations.description: The AlliMed Explorer, with the tagline “Soaring Miles through Responsible Journalism”, is the official media outlet of the College of Allied Medical Professions that covers all collegiate and departmental events for the academic year. It serves to discover, hone and exhibit the talents of allied health students in literary works, cartooning and multimedia journalism and in editorial, news, features and sports writing.

studentOrganizations.name: Alliance of Radiologic Technology Students (ARTS)
studentOrganizations.description: The ARTS aims to develop a responsible studentry who will put educational opportunity to optimum use and look after the unity and welfare of its members. It is geared towards developing a sense of service and responsibility among its members through activities that will benefit the school and the community while upholding the rules and regulations of the student handbook.

studentOrganizations.name: Capampangan Occupational Therapy Student Assembly (COTSA)
studentOrganizations.description: The Capampangan Occupational Therapy Student Assembly is the official organization of the AUF BS Occupational Therapy students. COTSA believes that student involvement and contribution in the community is important in the growing field of occupational therapy.

facilities.name: Physical Therapy Laboratory
facilities.description: Supported by different laboratories equipped with modalities and equipment to adequately meet the needs of the students in physical therapy and rehabilitation studies.
features: Hydrotherapy Area: Whirlpool (Extremity tank), Hydrocollator tank and packs, Paraffin wax bath
Traction Area: Intermittent Lumbar & Cervical Traction, Traction Table

Electrotherapy Area: Ultrasound, JOBST Intermittent Compression, Short Wave Diathermy, UVR (Ultraviolet Rays), Biofeedback Machine, Electrotherapeutic Devices (TENS, ED, FES), LASER, Interferential Therapy Apparatus

Therapeutic Exercise Laboratory: Finger Ladder, Shoulder Wheel, Vestibular Apparatus (wobble board, vestibular board and ball and other), Strengthening Equipment (ankle weights, dumbbells, thera bands)

facilities.name: Occupational Therapy Laboratory
facilities.description: A comprehensive laboratory containing evaluation tools, independent living simulators, pediatric treatment systems, and splinting tools.
features: OT Evaluation Tools: Purdue Pegboard, Minnesota Manual Dexterity Test, Jamar Hand Evaluation Kit, Box and Block Test, Minnesota Spatial Relations, Jebsen-Taylor Hand Function, Visual Perception Assessment, Feeding Evaluation, BaFPE, Goniometer Set
Therapeutic Skills Tools & Equipment: Cooking Equipments, Gas Range, Refrigerator, Sewing Equipment, Sewing Machine, Carpentry Tools, Hammer, Saw, Wedge
Independent Living Skills Equipment: Wheelchair, Wheelchair Accessories, Transfer Boards, Crutches, Canes, Patient Handling Sling

Pediatrics Treatment Tools/Equipment: Sensory Integration Equipment, Universal Grasshopper System, Tumble Forms, Vestibular Board, Variable Balance Beam, Sensory Integration Kit, Toys, Cognitoys, Body Parts Kit, Shape Sorter, Pediatric Puzzles, Lacing Shapes, Bead Mazes

Physical Dysfunction Treatment Tools: Exercise Mat, Plinths, Standing Table, Graded ROM Arc, Range of Reach, Extend Your Reach, Shoulder Exercise Ladder, Bilateral Sander, Stocking Cones, Exercise Skate, Wrist Roll, Theraputty, Digi-Flex, Pegboards, Activity Boards, Finger Extension, Perceptual Motor Program, Dressing Frame Set, Manipulation Board
Orthopedics Splinting Tools: Revolving Punch, Utility Knife, Silent Heat Gun, Rivet Gun, Compact Screw Driver, Splint Pan, Splint Pan Liner

facilities.name: Medical Technology Laboratory
facilities.description: A fully functional diagnostic lab environment with specialized sections for clinical chemistry, microscopy, blood banking, histology, and hematology.
features: Clinical Chemistry Section: Spectrophotometer, Water bath, Refrigerator, Micropipettes, Fume hoods
Bacteriology Section: Incubator, Autoclave, Oven Sterilizer, Inoculating loops and needles, Colony Counter, BioSafety Cabinet
Clinical Microscopy/Parasitology Section: Urinometer, Microscope, Centrifuge machine, Specimen Slides
Blood Banking/Immunohematology Section; Immunology/Serology: Shakers/Stirrers, Serofuge, Blood Bags
Hematology Section: Hemocytometer Sets, Hemoglobinometer Set, Microhematocrit Centrifuge, Westergen tube and rack, Wintrobe tube and rack

facilities.name: Pharmacy Laboratories
facilities.description: Specialized laboratories for pharmaceutical sciences, chemistry, quality control, and research technology, including a botanical garden and simulated pharmacy.
features: Pharmaceutical Sciences Laboratory: Analytical Balance, Blenders, Capsule Filters, Dessicators, Mixing Bowls, Ointment Slab, Oral Gastric Lavage tube, Ovens for Drying, Pill tile, Rectal/oral/digital thermometer, Sieves, Suppository molders (adult and pedia), Sterilizers, Tableting Machine

Pharmaceutical Chemistry Laboratory: Aspirators, Beakers, Buchner funnels, Burette (acid/base), Distilling Apparatus, Erlenmeyer flasks, Evaporating dish, Forceps, Funnels, Fume hoods, Glass rods, Graduated cylinder, Hot plates, Hydrometers, Magnifying glass, Micropipettes, Mohr’s Westphal balance, Mortar and Pestles, Petri dishes, Pipettes, Ph meters, Porcelain crucibles, Pycnometers, Separatory funnels, Spatula, Spray bottles, Stirring rods/Stirrers, Test tubes/racks/brush, Thermometers, Top Loading Balance, Tripods, Volumetric flask, Watch glass, Water baths, Westphal balance

Quality Control/ Instrumentation: Disintegration & Dissolution Apparatus, Melting Point apparatus, Moisture Analyzer, Muffle furnace, Polarimeter, Pyrogen Test, Refractometer, Tablet Friabilator, Tablet Hardness Tester, Thin Layer Chromatography, Vernier Calipers, Viscometer
Research Technology: Metabolic cage, Soxhlet Apparatus, Percolators, UV-VIS Spectrophotometer

name: College of Arts and Science
description: The College of Arts and Science provides a broad liberal arts and sciences education, developing well-rounded graduates prepared for leadership and service in diverse fields.
vision: The College of Arts and Sciences envisions to be the fountainhead for the preservation of culture, the propagation of the arts, and the promotion of science and technology through an ambiance of academic excellence.
mission: Cognizant of its vision, the College of Arts and Sciences is dedicated to promote quality liberal education and professional education in Biology, Psychology and Communication.

programs.name: Bachelor of Science in Biology and Bachelor of Science in Biology 3-Year Accelerated
programs.description: The Bachelor of Science in Biology and Bachelor of Science in Biology 3-Year Accelerated degree program aims to:
curriculum: Demonstrate an in-depth understanding of mechanisms and processes governing life
Perform mathematical and statistical computations to analyze biological data
Exhibit proficiency in communicating scientific information and ideas both orally and in writing
Utilize biological theories and the scientific method in understanding the relationship between structure and functions in the molecular cellular and organismal level
Apply biorisk management principles
Develop sustainable solutions to today’s environmental and social problems
Conduct biological research and critically assess current views and theories for the expansion of scientific knowledge

programs.name: Bachelor of Science in Human Biology
programs.description: The Bachelor of Science in Human Biology degree program aims to:
curriculum: Demonstrate understanding on the fundamental concepts of human biology, including cellular structure and function, genetics and development, metabolism, and physiological systems.
Explain the principles of chemical and molecular basis of function and disease.
Identify the etiology of diseases.
Integrate knowledge across organ systems to understand the health and wellness of the human body.
Develop problem-solving skills in the context of human biology
Demonstrate proficiency in laboratory and biorisk management techniques relevant to human biology
Evaluate and discuss the implications of research in the field of human biology

Demonstrate a set of skills embodying the Angelenean attributes of being values-oriented, socially and ethically responsible, professionally competent, critical and creative thinker, lifelong learner, and globally oriented.

programs.name: Bachelor of Arts in Communication
programs.description: The Bachelor of Arts In Communication degree program aims to:
curriculum: Define and access information needs; assess and organize information and knowledge produce, share and utilize information and knowledge
Communicate in different formats and platforms (print, broadcast, and online)
Prepare communication/ media plan
Conduct communication and media research and evaluation
Develop and produce communication materials in different formats and platforms
Demonstrate communication management and leadership skills
Develop entrepreneurial capabilities
Adhere to ethical standards and practices
Know and practice rights and responsibilities and accountabilities in the communication profession
Demonstrate a development orientation in communication work
Apply communication theories and models, principles, and practices and tools in development work

programs.name: Bachelor of Science in Psychology
programs.description: The Bachelor of Science In Psychology degree program aims to:
curriculum: Understand and apply theories and principles in the various fields of Psychology.
Conduct psychological evaluation through integrating data from psychological testing, interview, observation.
Prepare and implement structured learning experiences for different settings in Psychology.
Practice empathy, responsibility, and genuine concern as well as multi-cultural sensitivity, when dealing with problems, issues, needs and concerns of individuals.
Demonstrate ethical and professional standards in research and practice of Psychology
Apply different investigative methods in Psychology.
Develop and enhance competencies through further studies.

programs.name: Straight Bachelor of Arts in Psychology to Master of Arts in Psychology (SABMA)
programs.description: The Straight Bachelor of Arts In Psychology To Master Of Arts In Psychology program aims to:
curriculum: Demonstrate theoretical knowledge in Clinical Psychology.
Demonstrate knowledge of advanced methods of psychological inquiry in Clinical Psychology
Conduct psychological assessment through integrating data from various sources such as psychological testing, clinical interview, and behavioral observations.
Formulate and implement psychological interventions through individual and group counseling and psychotherapy.
Demonstrate ethical and professional standards in research and practice of Clinical Psychology
Continue to advance knowledge and competencies related to theory, practice, and research in Clinical Psychology

goals: Provide a general education program which is relevant and responsive to the needs of the local as well as global community;
Equip students with knowledge, skills, and attitudes necessary in the practice of their respective professions;
Cultivate in the students a research - oriented attitude and involvement in community activities which will help prepare them for productive a life in society;
Create an atmosphere of professionalism founded on integrity and ethical standards; and
Prepare the students for more specialized studies in the fields of Biology, Psychology, and Communication.

studentOrganizations.name: SAMASKOM
studentOrganizations.description: SAMASKOM is a non-mandated organization that initiates Communication-related activities in order to promote unity and develop camaraderie among all AB Communication students in the College.

studentOrganizations.name: Kapisanan ng mga Sikolohista sa Angeles University Foundation (KASAUF)
studentOrganizations.description: Kapisanan ng mga Sikolohista sa Angeles University Foundation (KASAUF) is a non-mandated organization which promotes social responsibility among members by providing them opportunities to serve AUF and the community. It aims to promote the physical, moral and psychological well-being of its members.

studentOrganizations.name: Biological Sciences Society (BSS)
studentOrganizations.description: Biological Sciences Society (BSS) is a non-mandated university-based organization that initiates and promotes awareness on environmental issues and current trends in Biology. It aims to train students become good stewards of the environment while appreciating scientific advancement.

name: College of Business and Accountancy
description: The College of Business Administration prepares future business leaders with a strong foundation in management, finance, marketing, and entrepreneurship guided by ethical principles.
vision: The College of Business and Accountancy envisions to be a premier business school in the region producing globally competitive, socially responsible and Christ-centered business professionals.
mission: The College of Business and Accountancy is committed to providing dynamic, high quality and holistic business education through excellence in instruction, research, and community engagement anchored on Catholic values.

programs.name: Bachelor of Science in Accountancy (BSA)
programs.description: The Bachelor of Science in Accountancy (BSA) program aims to produce competent and ethical professional accountants who can:
curriculum: Resolve business issues and problems with a global and strategic perspective using knowledge from financial accounting and reporting, cost management, accounting information systems, auditing, taxation, and business laws;
Formulate management decisions through quantitative and qualitative analysis of financial and non-financial data;
Demonstrate proficiency in oral and written communication skills appropriate for professional business environments;
Apply information technology and accounting software proficiently in financial reporting and auditing processes;
Conduct research in accounting and related fields to address emerging industry issues and trends;
Uphold professional ethics, social responsibility, and corporate governance standards in the practice of the accountancy profession; and
Pass the Certified Public Accountant (CPA) Licensure Examination and engage in lifelong learning for continuous professional development.

programs.name: Bachelor of Science in Management Accounting (BSMA)
programs.description: The Bachelor of Science in Management Accounting (BSMA) program aims to produce graduates who can:
curriculum: Utilize financial and non-financial information to plan, monitor, and control business operations and support strategic decision-making;
Design and implement management control systems, performance measurement metrics, and risk management frameworks;
Prepare, analyze, and interpret financial statements, budgets, and cost accounting reports for internal and external stakeholders;
Apply information technology, business intelligence tools, and accounting info systems proficiently in corporate operations;
Demonstrate effective communication, teamwork, and leadership skills in diverse organizational settings;
Comply with professional code of ethics, corporate governance principles, and relevant business laws; and
Pursue professional certifications (e.g., Certified Management Accountant) and engage in lifelong learning to adapt to a changing global business environment.

programs.name: Bachelor of Science in Business Administration major in Financial Management
programs.description: The Bachelor of Science in Business Administration major in Financial Management program aims to produce graduates who can:
curriculum: Analyze financial statements, market trends, and economic indicators to assess corporate performance and investment opportunities;
Formulate capital budgeting, working capital management, and capital structure decisions to maximize organizational value;
Evaluate investment portfolios, financial derivatives, and risk management strategies in volatile market conditions;
Utilize financial technology, analytical tools, and corporate modeling systems proficiently in investment and financial operations;
Exhibit effective communication, negotiation, and strategic collaboration skills in corporate and financial institutions;
Adhere to professional ethics, corporate social responsibility, and regulatory frameworks governing the financial sectors; and
Pursue professional career paths and certifications in corporate finance, banking, and wealth management while embracing lifelong learning.

programs.name: Bachelor of Science in Business Administration major in Marketing Management
programs.description: The Bachelor of Science in Business Administration major in Marketing Management program aims to produce graduates who can:
curriculum: Formulate integrated marketing communication strategies, product placement designs, and pricing models based on consumer behavior analysis;
Conduct comprehensive market research and analysis to identify emerging business trends, target markets, and competitive advantages;
Design and implement digital marketing campaigns, social media strategies, and e-commerce platforms to drive brand equity and customer engagement;
Utilize marketing analytics, CRM software, and data-driven intelligence tools proficiently in marketing metrics reporting;
Demonstrate superior interpersonal, professional communication, presentation, and leadership skills in corporate and entrepreneurial settings;
Practice corporate social responsibility, consumer protection values, and marketing ethics across local and international operations; and
Build successful careers in brand management, digital marketing, sales management, and market research through continuous professional development.

programs.name: Bachelor of Science in Hospitality Management (BSHM)
programs.description: The Bachelor of Science in Hospitality Management (BSHM) program aims to produce graduates who can:
curriculum: Demonstrate operational competencies in food and beverage production, front office management, housekeeping operations, and event coordination based on industry standards;
Apply management principles, financial controls, legal compliance, and human resource strategies effectively in hospitality establishments;
Deliver high-quality guest services, customer relationship management, and service recovery strategies with cultural sensitivity;
Utilize hospitality-specific information systems, point-of-sale software, and technological solutions proficiently in hospitality workflows;
Exhibit excellent communication, problem-solving, teamwork, and leadership skills in diverse hospitality environments;
Practice high ethical standards, environmental sustainability, and corporate social responsibility in global hospitality operations; and
Pursue continuous professional certifications, executive career ladders, and entrepreneurial opportunities in lodging, food service, and recreation sectors.

programs.name: Bachelor of Science in Tourism Management (BSTM)
programs.description: The Bachelor of Science in Tourism Management (BSTM) program aims to produce graduates who can:
curriculum: Formulate sustainable tourism plans, destination marketing strategies, and tourism policy frameworks aligned with socio-cultural, economic, and environmental goals;
Manage travel agency operations, tour guiding services, transport services, and corporate itinerary planning based on international guidelines;
Conduct tourism research, heritage mapping, and market segmentation analysis to address emerging developments and innovations in global travel industries;
Utilize global distribution systems (GDS), airline reservation software, and digital travel platforms proficiently in tourism operations;
Demonstrate advanced communication, cross-cultural diplomacy, public relations, and leadership skills across global tourism platforms;
Champion cultural heritage preservation, environmental protection, ecotourism values, and ethical business codes in travel industries; and
Establish specialized careers, professional certifications, and entrepreneurial ventures in destination management, travel consultancy, and airline industries.

programs.name: BSBA major in Human Resource Management

history: The College of Business and Accountancy started its existence in 1962 with the founding of Angeles Institute of Technology. It was initially named College of Commerce with an enrollment of thirty-three (33) students. It was in July 1963 that the college was given government recognition (DECS No. 70, Series 1963) to offer the first and second year programs of the Bachelor of Science in Commerce. In 1966-67, the college graduated the first batch of five students.

The dynamism in the field of business expanded the college curricular offering, and it was in 1969 that the Department of Education granted recognition for the conferment of the degree of Bachelor of Science in Business Administration (Cert. No. GR. 150) major in Accounting, Banking and Finance, Economics, and Management and Marketing.

The expanded curricular activity changed the name of the college to College of Commerce and School for Secretaries with the integration of the Secretarial program in 1980. It was further renamed to College of Business and Economics in 1984 with the Computer Center added in the following year. In 1989, it was named College of Business Administration with the separation of the School for Secretaries and the Computer Center, the objective of which is to give these respective programs autonomy in the attainment of their respective growth potentials.

In 1992, the college was given government recognition to offer a Bachelor of Science in Accountancy. Realizing the multi-disciplinary requirements of the industry, the college offered the Bachelor of Science in Tourism Management and Bachelor of Science in Hotel and Restaurant Management in school year 2005-2006. From its humble beginning of 33 students, the College has grown into one of the largest colleges in the university.

Currently, the College of Business and Accountancy has PAASCU Level IV reaccredited status for its Business Administration Programs. The Accountancy, Hospitality Management, and Tourism Management programs are PAASCU Level III re-accredited.
goals: Instill among students sound moral values, social responsibility and Christian virtues essential in the practice of their profession;
Provide students with high quality knowledge, technical and analytical skills responsive to the needs of the local and global business environments;
Provide students with varied interactive and experiential learning opportunities to enhance creativity, innovation and leadership qualities;
Encourage students to embrace lifelong learning, professional ethics and a passion for research and development; and
Inspire students to actively participate in community development activities that promote sustainability and uplift human dignity.

studentOrganizations.name: Junior Philippine Institute of Accountants (JPIA) – AUF Chapter
studentOrganizations.description: The official professional organization for Accountancy and Management Accounting students. It provides platforms for academic competitions, technical seminars, leadership workshops, and community outreach, preparing members for professional certification and corporate excellence.

studentOrganizations.name: Junior Financial Executives (JFINEX) – AUF Chapter
studentOrganizations.description: The academic organization for Financial Management students. It aims to broaden financial literacy, organize investment forums, manage trading simulations, and engage with banking and financial experts to keep students updated on modern financial markets.

studentOrganizations.name: Junior Marketing Association (JMA) – AUF Chapter
studentOrganizations.description: The academic organization for Marketing Management students. It cultivates creative and analytical talents through advertising exhibits, digital marketing forums, brand competitions, and networking with marketing professionals to expose students to corporate landscapes.

studentOrganizations.name: Hospitality Management Society (HMS)
studentOrganizations.description: The official student organization for Hospitality Management students. It organizes food conventions, culinary showcases, mixology challenges, bar service competitions, and corporate networking events to nurture hands-on mastery in hospitality industries.

studentOrganizations.name: Tourism Management Society (TMS)
studentOrganizations.description: The official student organization for Tourism Management students. It spearheads tour guiding competitions, destination planning seminars, cultural heritage exposures, eco-tourism projects, and flight cabin simulations to develop global standards in travel services.

facilities.name: Mock Hotel Room
facilities.description: Features an exact replication of a standard premium hotel room setting, designed to train students in real-world housekeeping standards, room maintenance workflows, guest check-in/out scenarios, and general accommodation operations.

facilities.name: Travel Bureau Room
facilities.description: A simulated travel agency and flight operations workspace. Equipped with terminal workstations, global distribution software (GDS), airline reservation systems, and an integrated airplane cabin mock-up workspace to train students in corporate ticketing, itinerary generation, destination marketing, and flight cabin protocols.

facilities.name: Hotel Front Desk Workspace
facilities.description: Designed to simulate the complete reception and front-office infrastructure of a first-class hotel establishment. Serves as the primary laboratory for practicing reservation management, guest relationship handling, billing, and front-office tech solutions.

facilities.name: Bar and Beverage Counter
facilities.description: Replicates the architecture and logistical setup of a commercial professional lounge bar. Provides a high-fidelity workspace for mastering hands-on beverage preparation, mixology techniques, barista workflows, wine storage, and professional bar hospitality standards.

facilities.name: Hospitality Management Kitchen Laboratory
facilities.description: Modeled precisely after first-class corporate hotels and professional production kitchens. This advanced workspace features a cold kitchen section, a hot kitchen workflow configuration, an integrated high-volume commercial food production workspace, specialized pastry/baking infrastructure, industrial-grade ovens, commercial refrigerators, and safety ventilation systems.

name: College of Criminal Justice Education
description: The College of Criminal Justice Education produces competent criminologists and justice professionals dedicated to upholding the rule of law and serving the community.
vision: The College of Criminal Justice Education envisions becoming a premier institution of criminal justice education, producing globally competent, ethically grounded, and Christ-centered professionals dedicated to public safety, justice, and community empowerment.
mission: The College of Criminal Justice Education is committed to providing premium education in criminology and forensic sciences through advanced instruction, innovative research, progressive community partnerships, and specialized tactical training anchored on Catholic values and professional ethics.

programs.name: Bachelor of Science in Criminology
programs.description: The Bachelor of Science in Criminology degree program aims to produce graduates who can:
curriculum: Demonstrate technical competence and foundational knowledge in criminal law, law enforcement administration, crime detection and investigation, forensic sciences, correctional administration, and criminology research;
Perform accurate criminal detection, forensic data documentation, and specialized evidence investigation workflows using modern instrumentation aligned with legal protocols;
Execute institutional or non-institutional correctional interventions and rehabilitation programs centered on human rights and ethical accountability;
Exhibit proficient communication skills, multi-professional collaboration abilities, and professional leadership in diverse public safety agencies;
Design data-driven crime prevention programs, strategic community policing strategies, and public safety risk management frameworks; and
Uphold professional code of ethics, constitutional mandates, and civic values while striving for continuous professional development and passing the Criminologist Licensure Examination.

history: In 1979, the College of Arts and Sciences offered the Bachelor of Science in Criminology degree as one of its academic programs. It was in 1982 when the College of Criminology was established as a separate college with Prof. Jose B. Maniwang as founding dean. Under his leadership, the College of Criminology was granted recognition on June 18, 1984 by the Department of Education, Culture and Sports pursuant to DECS Order No. 23, s. 1984.

The College started with only seventy nine (79) students in 1979 and its enrolment gradually increased in the following years. It has produced a total of eight (8) topnotchers since 1990 and has graduated more than a thousand students who are now gainfully employed as law enforcement officers, laboratory technicians, signature verifiers, security officers, criminal investigators in various private and public offices in the Philippines and abroad.

Today, AUF's BS Criminology program is the only PAASCU Level 3 Reaccredited program and the only Center of Excellence in Central Luzon. The AUF CRIM program is recognized in both regional and national levels such as Center for Human Rights Education in Region 3 and a top performing school in the February 2025 Criminologist Licensure Examination. The AUF Criminology passing rate exceeds the national average for multiple consecutive years. These awards and distinctions demonstrate the program's strong commitment to academic excellence, effective instructions, relevant and dynamic curriculum and premium laboratory equipment and facilities.
goals: Foster deep moral values, civic consciousness, and Christian values required for ethical and transparent governance in public safety and criminal justice systems;
Equip students with advanced theoretical knowledge, critical thinking, and technical competencies necessary for modern law enforcement, scientific crime detection, and forensic analysis;
Provide comprehensive operational, hands-on, and field-based experiential training activities to build professional leadership and tactical readiness;
Instill a culture of ongoing professional growth, legal excellence, adherence to human rights standards, and collaborative scientific research; and
Mobilize progressive community development advocacies, public safety awareness, crime prevention frameworks, and civic interventions that uplift human dignity.

studentOrganizations.name: Criminology Student Council (CSC)
studentOrganizations.description: The official student governing body of the college. It spearheads student representation, academic-administrative coordination, leadership assemblies, and institutional welfare projects to advance the collective goals of the student body.

studentOrganizations.name: AUF Crimson Shield Society
studentOrganizations.description: A specialized academic-tactical student organization focused on advancing crime investigation skills, procedural law drills, specialized disaster management protocols, public security administration field studies, and civic safety advocacies.

studentOrganizations.name: Forensic Science Society (FSS)
studentOrganizations.description: The professional student collective for criminology students specialized in forensic science workflows. It coordinates advanced workshops on latent print development, forensic chemistry, question document verifications, ballistics indexing, and simulated crime scene processing workflows.

facilities.name: Crime Scene Room
facilities.description: A specialized laboratory designed to simulate realistic mock crime scenes. It serves as an active learning center where students apply physical security techniques, secure areas, practice lifting and documentation of latent fingerprints, reconstruct crime trajectories, identify evidentiary tracking matrices, and practice real-world chain of custody processing protocols under rigorous forensic standards.

facilities.name: Forensic Chemistry and Toxicology Room
facilities.description: Equipped with advanced instrumentation setups for qualitative and quantitative micro-chemical testing workflows. It provides a technical testing facility for identifying chemical residues, blood splatter configurations, micro-biological compounds, narcotics classification, qualitative toxicological indexing, glass fracture behaviors, and specialized soil/powder examinations.

facilities.name: Questioned Document Room
facilities.description: Designed for structural penmanship, ink, and document analysis. This facility contains optical comparators, ultraviolet/infrared analysis lamps, and high-fidelity measurement systems to examine security paper fibers, handwriting variations, structural erasures, counterfeit currency profiles, and forgery detection matrices.

facilities.name: Polygraphy Room
facilities.description: A soundproof interrogation and biometric recording suite. It houses professional multi-channel electronic polygraph systems to monitor cardiosphygmograph variations, pneumograph activity, and galvanic skin resistances, training students in forensic interviewing methodologies and truth-verification tracking data.

facilities.name: Dactyloscopy Room
facilities.description: A fingerprint processing and archiving station. Features professional fingerprint taking kit benches, latent card indexing storage drawers, customized magnifying glasses, ridges counting tools, and electronic fingerprint scanners to master identification classification methods (Henry System) and comparison verification.

facilities.name: Forensic Ballistics Room
facilities.description: Contains technical displays of ammunition types, propellant profiles, firing pin configurations, and firearm classifications. Features a specialized bullet recovery box apparatus alongside comparison stereomicroscopes to analyze rifling marks, land and groove configurations, and bullet striation mapping data.

name: College of Computer Studies
description: The College of Computer Studies fosters innovation and technical excellence through programs in computer science, information technology, and information systems.
vision: The College of Computer Studies envisions to emerge as the center of excellence in IT education, research and community service in the region. It aims to  develop professionals who are globally competitive, socially responsible and dedicated to the advancement of the IT industry in the country and in the world.
mission: The College aims to provide well – rounded Information Technology Education (ITE) Programs to meet the standards for professional practitioners in the country and in the global community

programs.name: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
programs.description: The BS Computer Science program includes the study of computing concepts and theories, algorithmic foundations, and new developments in computing. The program prepares students to design and create algorithmically complex software and develop new and effective algorithms for solving computing problems. The program also includes the study of the standards and practices in Software Engineering. It prepares students to acquire skills and disciplines required for designing, writing and modifying software components, modules and applications that comprise software solutions.
curriculum: Data Structures and Algorithms
Discrete Mathematics for Computing
Operating Systems and Computer Architecture
Database Systems and Big Data
Software Engineering and Design Patterns
Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning
Computer Networks and Cybersecurity
Capstone Research Project

specializations.name: Data Science
specializations.description: Data Science is the field of study that covers the systematic analysis of data within a scientific framework. It utilizes scientific methods, algorithms, processes and systems to extract meaningful insights from data across interdisciplinary domains.

admissionRequirements: Senior High School graduate (STEM strand preferred)
Pass the AUF College Admission Test (AUFCAT)
Submit Form 138 and Certificate of Good Moral Character

programs.name: Bachelor of Science in Information Technology
programs.description: Bachelor of Science in Information Technology (BSIT) program includes the study of the utilization of both hardware and software technologies involving planning, installing, customizing, operating, managing and administering, and maintaining information technology infrastructure that provides computing solutions to address the needs of an organization. The program prepares graduates to address various user needs involving the selection, development, application, integration and management of computing technologies within an organization.
curriculum: Web Development (Full-Stack)
Mobile Application Development
Network and Systems Administration
Cloud Computing and DevOps
Information Assurance and Security
IT Project Management
Human-Computer Interaction
Internship and Capstone Project

specializations.name: Web and Mobile Technologies
specializations.description: The BSIT Major in Web and Mobile Application aims to give students the knowledge and abilities needed to build web applications as well as mobile applications. CCS aspires to equip students with the abilities required to be successful in such a position in order to meet that need. These competencies include:
bullets: Website development and design
UI/UX design
Computer Programming/Scripting
Technical Communication
Database Administration
Mobile Application Development

specializations.name: I.T. Infrastructure
specializations.description: The BSIT Major in I.T. Infrastructure aims to give students the knowledge and abilities needed to manage and maintain an organization's computer network. CCS seeks to equip students with the knowledge and abilities required to maintain an organization's computer network in a state of readiness in order to meet that demand. These competencies include:
bullets: Learning networking principles
Managing and maintaining servers, computers, routers, and switches
Network configuration, network processing, centralized and distributed network connection
Installing, configuring, and administering network technologies
Learning to troubleshoot CISCO networks and devices
Cloud computing
Cloud architecting

admissionRequirements: Pass the AUF College Admission Test (AUFCAT)
Submit Form 138 and Certificate of Good Moral Character

programs.name: Bachelor of Multimedia Arts
programs.description: The Bachelor of Multimedia Arts aligns itself to the rapid convergence of media technologies and practices by developing conceptual, technical, aesthetic and professional competencies for effective, critical and innovative storytelling across a range or combination of media forms for various purposes. The program is concentrated on the following skills and competencies:
curriculum: Graphic Design
Animation
admissionRequirements: Pass the AUF College Admission Test (AUFCAT)
Submit Form 138 and Certificate of Good Moral Character

goals: Facilitate the instructional process for a better understanding of ITE Programs through a curriculum relevant to the needs of the industry;

Provide the students with the necessary exposure in order to relate the theoretical knowledge obtained in the College to real practices in the use of computers in the world of Information Communication Technology (ICT);
Inculcate in the students the importance of the proper utilization of computer systems resources for the growth and advancement of Information Technology;
Instill in the minds of the students a high degree of professionalism and moral values necessary in the practice of profession.

studentOrganizations.name: Junior Philippine Computer Society (JPCS) — AUF Chapter
studentOrganizations.description: A recognized student organization for Information Technology and Computer Science students that promotes leadership, technical excellence, academic activities, and participation in technology-related events and competitions.

studentOrganizations.name: Honor Society (HonSoc)
studentOrganizations.description: An academic organization that recognizes and promotes scholastic excellence, leadership, professionalism, and student engagement among high-performing CCS students.

studentOrganizations.name: Multimedia and System Developers (MSD)
studentOrganizations.description: A student organization focused on multimedia production, creative design, system development, and collaboration on digital and technology-driven projects.

studentOrganizations.name: Datalink
studentOrganizations.description: A student-led publication and technology organization dedicated to communication, information dissemination, digital media, and student engagement within the College of Computer Studies.

activities.name: AUF CodeFest
activities.description: Annual 24-hour hackathon bringing together students, alumni, and industry mentors to build innovative software solutions.

activities.name: Dragons Tech Week
activities.description: A week-long celebration of computing — featuring industry talks, workshops, project showcases, and the annual capstone fair.

activities.name: Digital Literacy Outreach
activities.description: Quarterly community outreach teaching basic computer literacy to public school students in surrounding barangays.

activities.name: Industry Immersion Program
activities.description: Structured visits to local and Metro Manila tech companies, giving students first-hand exposure to professional software practices.

affiliations.name: Adobe Creative Cloud for Education
affiliations.description: Provides creative tools and digital design applications that help students develop industry-relevant multimedia and production skills.

affiliations.name: Animation Council of the Philippines Inc. (ACPI)
affiliations.description: Supports the promotion of Filipino animation talent locally and internationally while strengthening industry collaboration in 2D and 3D animation.

affiliations.name: Cisco Networking Academy
affiliations.description: Global IT and cybersecurity education program providing networking, cybersecurity, and certification training aligned with industry standards.

affiliations.name: CompTIA
affiliations.description: Provides access to industry-recognized certifications and training programs in cybersecurity, networking, cloud computing, and IT support.

affiliations.name: Huawei ICT Academy
affiliations.description: Promotes technological innovation and talent development through specialized training and certifications in 5G, AI, cloud computing, and IoT.

affiliations.name: Microsoft Learn for Educators
affiliations.description: Provides Microsoft Official Curriculum, teaching resources, and certification-aligned learning materials for students and faculty.

affiliations.name: Multimedia Arts Association of the Philippines (MMAAP)
affiliations.description: Professional organization that supports multimedia arts education, networking, workshops, seminars, and industry collaboration in the Philippines.

affiliations.name: Oracle Academy
affiliations.description: Provides Oracle software, curriculum resources, and certification pathways to help students gain industry-relevant database and enterprise technology skills.

affiliations.name: Philippine Society of Information Technology Educators (PSITE)
affiliations.description: Professional organization of IT educators focused on faculty development and promoting quality IT education in the Philippines.

affiliations.name: SAP University Alliance Program Council of the Philippines (SUCP)
affiliations.description: Provides universities with SAP software access, training opportunities, and enterprise system education aligned with global industry practices.

collegeAccreditations.description: Autonomous status granted by the Commission on Higher Education (CHED). To date, only 68 out of 2,393 Higher Educational Institutions (HEIs) are granted this status.
Institutional Accreditation by the Federation of Accrediting Associations of the Philippines (FAAP).

collegeAccreditations.program: BS Computer Science & BS Information Technology
collegeAccreditations.description: Level III Accreditation by the Philippine Accrediting Association of Schools, Colleges and Universities (PAASCU) for the Computer Science and Information Technology programs.

recognitions.title: Center of Excellence in I.T. Education
recognitions.description: Recognized by the Commission on Higher Education for excellence in Information Technology education, academic quality, research, and institutional performance.

recognitions.title: Awarded by the Commission on Higher Education
recognitions.description: Received official recognition from the Commission on Higher Education for outstanding contributions to higher education and academic development.

facilities.name: Computer Laboratories
facilities.description: Five computer laboratories equipped with individual workstations and Internet connectivity to support programming, multimedia, networking, and other computing-related activities.

facilities.name: Lecture Rooms
facilities.description: Fully air-conditioned lecture rooms designed to provide a comfortable and technology-supported learning environment for students.

facilities.name: Open Laboratory
facilities.description: An open-access laboratory that allows students to conduct research, complete coursework, and practice technical skills beyond class hours.

facilities.name: Hardware Laboratory
facilities.description: A specialized laboratory dedicated to computer hardware assembly, maintenance, troubleshooting, and hands-on technical training.

facilities.name: ICT Research and Development Laboratory
facilities.description: A research-oriented laboratory supporting innovation, system development, and ICT-related research projects of students and faculty members.
features: Collaboration area for students and faculty

facilities.name: Wi-Fi Access Facility
facilities.description: Wireless Internet access point available for students and faculty to support digital learning and online academic activities.

cta.headline: Build what's next.
cta.description: Join a community of dragons shaping the future of technology in the Philippines. Our doors are open to passionate learners.

name: College of Engineering and Architecture
description: The College of Engineering and Architecture develops skilled engineers and architects who apply scientific and technological knowledge to solve real-world problems with integrity and innovation.
vision: The College of Engineering and Architecture envisions to produce the country's builders of environment-friendly structures and systems.
mission: To realize its vision, the College is committed to develop responsive and responsible engineers and architects who shall become leaders and citizens of the twenty-first century.

programs.name: Bachelor of Science in Civil Engineering
Bachelor of Science in Computer Engineering
Bachelor of Science in Electronics Engineering

goals: Develop competent, productive and self-reliant engineers and architects imbued with sound moral values;
Conduct and encourage relevant and innovative researches in the field of engineering, architecture and technology;
Provide extension services that will contribute to community development in terms of skills training, engineering and other technical assistance;
Promote and maintain mutually beneficial linkages and networking with professional organizations, educational institutions and the industry; and
Generate goodwill and support of the alumni and the community through active involvement in their activities.

affiliations.name: Institute of Electronics and Communications Engineering of the Philippines
Association of Civil Engineering Educators of the Philippines
National Research Council of The Philippines
Philippine Association of Tertiary Level Educational Institutions in Environmental Protection and Management
Philippine Council for Industry and Energy Research and Development

name: College of Education
description: The College of Education shapes the future of learning by developing passionate, skilled, and values-driven educators committed to transformative teaching.
vision: The College of Education envisions to be the center for excellence in instruction, research, community service and other scholarly activities in teacher education, whose sphere of intellectual influence extends to both public and private schools.
mission: To realize its vision, the College is committed to develop Christian educators who are dedicated to help the youth to emerge as the nation’s partners of development and the protectors of human life and the environment.

programs.name: Bachelor of Elementary Education (BEEd)
programs.description: The Bachelor of Elementary Education (BEEd) degree program aims to:
curriculum: Prepare the prospective teachers to handle any grade in the elementary level with confidence, expertise, competence and dedication;
Enable the prospective teachers to direct and facilitate effectively the learning of pre-elementary and elementary pupils;
Assist the prospective teachers to acquire the necessary skills in guiding the character formation of pupils by serving as role models;
Promote students' interest in research and further studies as part of capability building in the teaching profession; and
Help the students become advocates of Christian principles, virtues, and values at all times, in campus and out campus.

programs.name: Bachelor of Secondary Education (BSEd)
programs.description: The Bachelor of Secondary Education (BSE) degree program aims to:
curriculum: Develop high school teachers who can teach in one of the different learning areas in high school such as, but not limited to Biological Sciences, English, Filipino, Mathematics, P.E.H.M among others;
Enable the prospective teachers to direct and facilitate effectively the learning of high school students;
Assist the prospective teachers in acquiring the necessary skills in guiding the character formation of students;
Promote students' interest in research and further studies as part of capability building in the teaching profession; and
Help the students become advocates of Christian principles, virtues, and values at all times, in campus and out campus.

history: The College of Education (CED) was established in 1962 as part of the College of Arts and Sciences (CAS) and was mainly tasked to handle undergraduate programs. In 1980, it was tasked by DECS to supervise private schools which were implementing the New Elementary Schools Curriculum (NESC) in Central Luzon. Two years later in 1983, it emerged as a separate unit and was selected by the Department of Education, Culture and Sports as a pilot college in Region III.

The College started offering Bachelor of Elementary Education (BEEd) in 1986 and Bachelor of Secondary Education (BSEd) in 1989. Specializations under the BEEd program include General Education, Preschool Education, and Special Education. The BSEd program offers eight areas of specialization namely, English, Filipino, Biological Sciences, General Science, Mathematics, Social Studies, Values Education, and MAPEH. In 2011, Chinese Language Teaching (CLT) was offered as a BSEd double-major program in partnership with the Confucius Institute and the Fujian Normal University, People’s Republic of China.
Today, the College is a Center of Excellence in Teacher Education, PAASCU Level IV Accredited and ISO 9001:2008 certified by TUV-SUD.

From a small unit in 1962 that gradually emerged as a formidable college, CED has produced topnotchers, passers in the Licensure Examination for Teachers (LET) and hundreds of graduates who are now gainfully employed as educators and education managers.
goals: Develop a deep love of country and an abiding faith in God and man;
Stimulate students' leadership potentials and assist them become effective change agents through specialized development programs;

Provide an academic environment which will open avenues to update students on local, national and global issues and concerns vis-à-vis the developments in science and technology and other matters in education; and
Equip students with sound pedagogical theories and strategies together with the corresponding skills to enable them to teach in the elementary and secondary levels of education.

studentOrganizations.name: Teacher’s Pen
studentOrganizations.description: This is the official publication of the College considered as the hub of writers, photojournalists, and cartoonists who keep the community updated, persuaded, and entertained.

studentOrganizations.name: ELNB Educational Leaders for Nation Building
studentOrganizations.description: ELNB is the community extension organization of the College.

studentOrganizations.name: Chinese Language Teaching (CLT) Society
studentOrganizations.description: The CLT Society promotes the appreciation and propagation of Chinese language, art, culture and history through education.

studentOrganizations.name: Meta Art
studentOrganizations.description: Artists from CED come home to MetArt where their skills and styles are developed and shared.

studentOrganizations.name: Metanoia Dance Troupe
studentOrganizations.description: This performing group brings out the creativity in the members. Students move the groove with workshops and projects in traditional and modern dance.

studentOrganizations.name: Metaeuphony Glee
studentOrganizations.description: This singing group unites music lovers with songs across all genres.

studentOrganizations.name: Kapisanang Itinatag sa Asignaturang Filipino (KISAF)
studentOrganizations.description: Ang KISAF ay organisasyong itinatag ng mga mag-aaral ng AUF na kumukuha ng asignaturang Filipino. Layunin nitong masanay ang mga mag-aaral sa kanilang kakayahang pagkamalikhain sa pamamagitan ng paggawa at pakikiisa sa mga proyekto o gawaing pampaaralan na may kinalaman sa pagpapalawak ng Wikang Filipino.

studentOrganizations.name: AUF English Club
studentOrganizations.description: The AUF English Club is open to all students taking up English courses. It spearheads academic contests and seminars to sharpen and develop the English language skills of members.

studentOrganizations.name: AUF PE Club
studentOrganizations.description: All PE students are welcome to this organization that provides opportunities for students to explore and enhance their physical prowess through dance, sports and recreational activities while developing positive social values with and for others.

studentOrganizations.name: Guardian Angel Society
studentOrganizations.description: This organization provides mentoring activities to Education students and spearheads seminars, exhibits and other meaningful projects.

topnotches.name: Ms. Pauline Joviene D. Quiazon
topnotches.exam: Licensure Examination for Teachers (LET) - BEEd Special Education

topnotches.name: Mr. Raymond B. Canlapan
topnotches.exam: Licensure Examination for Teachers (LET) - BSEd Math

topnotches.name: Ms. Lourdes Gonzales
topnotches.exam: Licensure Examination for Teachers (LET) - BSE Math

name: College of Nursing
description: The College of Nursing produces competent, compassionate, and Christ-centered nurses dedicated to promoting health and delivering quality patient care.
vision: The College of Nursing is the center for the promotion of quality nursing education, geared towards competence, compassion, and competitiveness in the global context.
mission: To realize its vision, the College is committed to the development of quality nursing practice dedicated towards disease prevention, health promotion, curative activities and rehabilitation.

programs.name: Bachelor of Science in Nursing
programs.description: The Bachelor of Science in Nursing program is a four-year program designed to prepare students to become professional nurses who are competent, compassionate, and Christ-centered in providing nursing care.

goals: Develop a sensitive awareness and understanding of physical, social, economic, cultural, and spiritual problems of society;
Acquire the necessary knowledge, skills, and attitudes for the improvement of health and promotion of quality of life;
Adopt a research oriented attitude through the use of the nursing process utilizing research findings for the improvement of health services;
Imbibe spiritual and ethical values in the provision of quality patient care; and
Enhance leadership potentials of students to function independently or in collaboration with others.

affiliations.name: Jose B. Lingad Memorial Regional Hospital

Partner Communities (Sitio Monicayo, Brgy. Lourdes Sur East, Brgy. Lourdes Northwest, Brgy. Sto. Domingo, Brgy. Pulungbulu, Brgy. Pulung Cacutud, Brgy. Balibago, Brgy. Mining, Brgy. Sapa Libutad, Brgy. Ninoy Aquino, Brgy. Cutcut, Brgy. San Jose, and Diosdado Macapagal Village)
Ricardo P. Rodriguez Memorial District Hospital

facilities.name: Nursing Arts Laboratory
facilities.description: Four air-conditioned Nursing Arts Laboratories equipped with facilities for demonstration of basic nursing procedures, simulating hospital wards, operating rooms, delivery rooms, nurseries, and emergency settings.
features: Adjacent hospital simulation room (beds, OR, DR, nursery, emergency room)
Accommodates up to sixty (60) students per laboratory

facilities.name: Nutrition Laboratories
facilities.description: Practice areas for Level II nursing students, containing dedicated working, storage, and dining areas.
features: Working area: intended for cooking; equipped with a sink and faucet
Storage area: holds refrigerator and cabinets for dinner wares and utensils
Dining area: consists of a dining table (1 x 22m) and ten (10) dining chairs

facilities.name: Anatomy and Physiology Rooms
facilities.description: Fully air-conditioned rooms used for lecture and demonstration purposes, containing human skeletal, muscular, and organ models.
features: Diverse human anatomy models (skeletal, muscular, and organ systems)
Accommodates 50 students at any given time

name: School of Law
description: The School of Law produces lawyers and legal professionals committed to justice, ethical practice, and the rule of law in service of the Filipino people.
vision: The AUF School of Law envisions to emerge as the center of excellence in legal education north of Metro Manila.
mission: In keeping with Christian values and virtues, the AUF School of Law is committed to providing quality legal education that is accessible to students in the Central Luzon area, committed to the observance of the highest standard of professional conduct and competencies, and responsive to technological advances, globalization, and socio-economic conditions.
history: The AUF School of Law was established in response to the clamor for quality legal education in Central Luzon. The establishment of the School earned approval from the AUF Board of Trustees on August 19, 2003 during its 56th Regular Meeting presided over by Pampanga Archbishop Paciano B. Aniceto, D.D., in his capacity as Chairman of the Board. The approval was followed by immense planning and preparation that spanned three years, which included the construction of the AUF Professional Schools Building, now home to the School.

The Commission on Higher Education, upon the recommendation of the Technical Panel for Legal Education, resolved to grant AUF the permit to operate the School of Law on March 13, 2006. Under the leadership of Retired Supreme Court Justice Jose C. Vitug as Founding Dean and Atty. Domingo M. Navarro as Vice Dean, the School boasts a roster of faculty members composed of jurists and distinguished law professors. The School is equipped with a modern law library, a moot court, and state-of-the-art facilities that provide a conducive teaching and learning environment.

The School of Law received a Certificate of Recognition from the Legal Education Board for its notable performance in the 2012, 2013, and 2014 Bar Examinations, as certified by the Office of the Bar Confidant. It was listed as the top performing law school among those with 19 or fewer examinees. Today, the School of Law is consistently among the Top 10 law schools nationwide and the No. 1 in Northern and Central Luzon.
goals: Prepare students for the practice of law.
Produce competent and committed members of the legal profession imbued with sound social conscience.
Train students for national leadership.
Contribute to the advancement of the country’s legal and judicial systems in the light of current national and international developments.

admissions.intro: Applicants for the Juris Doctor (JD) degree must satisfy the following requirements:

groups.title: Juris Doctor Admission Requirements
items: A holder of a four-year bachelor’s degree from a duly recognized institution, having earned at least 18 units in English, 6 units in Mathematics, and 18 units in Social Science (History, Political Science, Economics, Philosophy, Psychology, etc.); a holder of a Bachelor of Science or Arts degree with deficiencies of not more than 9 units in these subjects may still be admitted if the units of deficiency are taken during the first curriculum year.
Applicant must have obtained a general weighted average of at least 85%, subject to the provisions below.
Applicant must successfully pass the AUF Law Admissions Test, which includes two essays on topics determined by the School.
Applicant must hurdle an evaluation interview before the Admissions Committee.

An applicant who does not meet the required minimum general weighted average of 85% but produces satisfactory essays and possesses a satisfactory character reference may be admitted “on probation” if he or she passes the AUF Law Admissions Test and the evaluation interview; a probationary student may apply for a change of status to regular after completing the first semester with an average of at least 78% and no failing or incomplete grades, subject to evaluation by the School Dean on recommendation of the Admissions Committee.
Transfer credits may be allowed on a case-to-case basis at the discretion of the Dean, in consultation with the School of Law Admissions Committee.

admissions.note: Applicants are given a test schedule upon compliance with all documentary requirements.

roster: Presiding Judge Ed Vincent S. Albano
Prof. Ma. Tanya Karina A. Lat
Prof. Edgardo Carlo L. Vistan II

name: School of Medicine
description: The School of Medicine is committed to producing medical doctors of the highest caliber — scientists, clinicians, and humanitarians who serve with excellence and compassion.
vision: The School of Medicine envisions itself as a center for quality and relevant medical education as evidenced by its programs and services recognized locally and internationally, resulting in the total satisfaction of its customers and other stakeholders around the world.
mission: To realize its vision, the School of Medicine is committed to produce quality and compassionate medical practitioners imbued with a social conscience and dedication to the service of God and humanity.
history: The Angeles University Foundation School of Medicine (AUF-SOM) was established in June 1983 by the authority of the Board of Medical Education and the Department of Education, Culture, and Sports with Dr. Rodolfo C. Dimayuga as founding dean, Dr. Presentacion C. Peralta as assistant dean and concurrently school secretary, and a faculty of 18, eight of whom boast of 20 years teaching experience. The AUF-SOM was initially housed in the AUF San Lorenzo Ruiz Building until it was gutted down by fire in 1993. This tragedy gave way to the construction of a new health sciences building which now houses the AUF-SOM office, lecture rooms and laboratories.

The AUF-SOM was solely affiliated with Jose B. Lingad Memorial Regional Hospital for the clinical training of students until the establishment of the AUF Medical Center on February 23, 1990. The 125-bed AUFMC is the only private tertiary teaching, training and research hospital in Central Luzon which serves as the base hospital of AUF medical and allied medical students. Acknowledged for their expertise and integrity, the AUF-SOM faculty play an active role in elevating the standards of Philippine medical education through their involvement as accreditors, technical panelists and board specialty examiners.

The AUF Doctor of Medicine program is the only PAASCU accredited program in Central Luzon. It was the first in the region given ISO 9001-2000 certification by TUV-SUD in 2008 and the only one north of Manila that is ISO 9001-2008 certified by TUV-SUD.

The AUF Doctor of Medicine program has produced outstanding board performance in the Philippine medical board exam, stellar performance at the Indian Medical Council Screening Test, and a 100% pass rate in the 2014 Nepal Medical Council Screening Test. As a result, an increased number of Indian students are now enrolled in the AUF BS Psychology program leading to the degree Doctor of Medicine.
goals: Prepare students to practise medicine in the service of God and humanity.
Instill in students the human qualities of dedication to duty, kindness, patience and compassion.
Encourage and maintain awareness on social and economic conditions of the country and of the world at large.
Equip students with adequate knowledge and skills in basic medical sciences, the clinical disciplines, and preventive medicine.
Inspire students to keep abreast with developments, innovations and researches in the field of medicine.
Motivate students to pursue lifelong learning in their field of specialization.

groups.title: Minimum Requirements
items: Applicant must be a holder of any baccalaureate degree.
Applicant must have taken the National Medical Admission Test (NMAT) and obtained a score of 40% or higher.

groups.title: Application Requirements (scanned image files, for initial evaluation)
items: Application for Admission (AUF Form SOM-01)
Official Bachelor’s Degree Transcript of Records
Diploma or Certificate of Graduation
NMAT Result

groups.title: Foreign Students — Additional Documents
items: Passport
Birth certificate
Medical fitness certificate
Police clearance

admissions.note: The following must be prepared (not emailed) and submitted, together with the originals of all previously emailed documents, on the date of psychometric testing: a Certification of General Weighted Average, and two Good Moral Character certifications — one from the Dean of the undergraduate course and one from either the Guidance Counselor or the Office of Student Affairs — each submitted in a sealed envelope.

studentOrganizations.name: Medical Student Council
studentOrganizations.description: The AUF SOM Medical Student Council serves as the collective voice of the student body, inspiring innovation, fostering cooperation, providing a forum for ideas, and promoting action to empower students to become the best physicians of the future.

studentOrganizations.name: Angeles Medical Surgical Mission Society (AMSMS)
studentOrganizations.description: The community service organization of medical students aimed at providing free health care to poor patients.

name: Graduate School
description: The Graduate School offers advanced degree programs fostering research, leadership, and professional mastery for working professionals and aspiring academics.
vision: The Graduate School envisions to be the leading higher education institution in the region offering graduate programs that are responsive to the professional and executive development needs of the human resources of corporate entities and educational institutions in the private and public sectors.
mission: The AUF Graduate School aims to produce high-level professionals capable of meeting the national and international needs in the dynamic and competitive global market through the provision of graduate and continuing professional education programs anchored on advanced theories, modern technologies, best practices and current trends in the various fields.
